content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 18, 1987

Ms. Jane Dolan
Supervisor, District 2
Butte County
P.O. Box 3700
Chico, CA 95927

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-87-068

Dear Ms. Dolan:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign reporting
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

FACTS

You are a County Supervisor in District 2, Butte County. You
will not be up for re-election during 1987. Your controlled
committee ended 1986 with no outstanding loans or debts.

QUESTION

Is there a difference in filing requirements if you raise
money during 1987 versus not raising any money?

CONCLUSION

Your filing requirements for 1987 are not determined by
whether or not you raise money. As an elected officeholder with a
controlled committee, you are required to file a Form 490,
"Candidate and Officeholder Campaign Statement-Long Form and
Consolidated Campaign Statement," semi-annually whether or not you
have received any contributions or made any expenditures during
the six-month period covered by the statement. The periods
covered by semi-annual statements are January 1 through June 30
and July 1 through December 31, with filing deadlines of July 31
and January 31, respectively. (Government Code Section 84200.)

You are not required to file regular pre-election statements
during any year in which you are not being voted upon.

1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Ms. Jane Dolan
Page 2


     There are two special situations in which officeholders who
are not required to file regular pre-election statements may be
required to file special campaign statements during a non-election
year.  These statements are required to be filed if you make
contributions or independent expenditures to support or oppose
other officeholders, candidates or ballot measures.  Please refer
to Part IV, pages 20 and 21, of the enclosed 1987 "Information
Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform
Act," for a detailed explanation of these important filing
requirements.

     If you have any additional questions concerning this matter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                   By:   Mary Ann Kvasager
                                        Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

DMG:MAK:kmt

JANE DOLAN
Supervisor, District 2, Butte County
Office: County Building ■ 196 Memorial Way ■ Chico, California
Mail: P.O. Box 3700 ■ Chico, California 95927 ■ (916)891-2830

February 23, 1987


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms.

As a County Supervisor who is not facing an
election this year, what are my campaign filing
requirements?

I have one controlled committee (Friends of Jane
Dolan) and filed in January of 1987, covering
through the end of 1986.

I have no outstanding loans or debts.

Is there a difference in filing requirements if
I raise money this year versus not raising any
money?

Thank you in advance for your response.

Sincerely,

Jane Dolan
Supervisor, County of Butte

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


February 27, 1987


Jane Dolan
Butte County Supervisor
P.O. Box 3700
Chico, CA  95927

Re:  87-068

Dear Ms. Dolan:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on February 27, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days.  You also should be aware that your
letter and our response are public records which may be
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
